<!-- generated by bootstrap-agents-md v1.1.0 -->

# Lessons Learned

Hard-won knowledge from building this codebase. When you make a mistake or discover a non-obvious behavior, add it here.

## General / Tooling

- `catalog/` is excluded from `tsconfig.json` — agent `.ts` files there won't typecheck and aren't supposed to; don't "fix" their imports
- This repo runs Next.js 16: `params` in pages and route handlers is a `Promise` and must be awaited; consult `node_modules/next/dist/docs/` before writing framework code
- Dynamic route paths contain brackets (`app/agents/[slug]/`) — quote them in zsh commands
- Official eve.dev integration pages SSR Install/Quick start/Configure in `<main>`, but Streamdown code blocks are also duplicated in streamed `S:N` slots — scrapers must dedupe by `(language, code)` and preserve code-line indentation (don't collapse whitespace inside fenced blocks)
- Fumadocs content must live under `content/docs/`, not `docs/` — the latter is reserved for agent/ops plans; pointing `defineDocs` at `docs/` would publish internal plans as public pages
- After installing or changing Fumadocs collections, run `fumadocs-mdx` (via `pnpm typecheck` / `postinstall`) so `collections/server` resolves from `.source/`
- `includeProcessedMarkdown: true` on the docs collection is required for `page.data.getText("processed")` used by `/llms.txt` and `/llms.mdx`; regenerate `.source/` after toggling it
- Keep nav, GitHub URL, and `/agents.md` instructions in `lib/site.ts` — do not hardcode duplicates in header/footer/docs layout
- Geistdocs look without the package: put Geist `--ds-*` values in `app/globals.css`, map shadcn `--background`/`--card`/… onto them, remap Tailwind `--shadow-*` in `@theme` to Geist materials, and keep product aliases `.shadow-surface` / `.shadow-chip` — so stock shadcn `shadow-lg` and our listing cards share one elevation system
- Material shadows already paint the 1px edge (`--ds-shadow-border-*`). Keep `border-transparent` only as a focus slot; never pair a visible `border-border` with `shadow-chip`/`shadow-surface`. Integration marks use the Badge chip recipe (`IntegrationBadge` / `IntegrationLink`), not Button outline, so cards and detail share one elevation/radius.
- Do not put `shadow-chip` on solid primary fills — the light `--ds-shadow-background-border` ring muddies dark CTAs. Use `.shadow-button` / `--ds-shadow-button` (inset highlight + contact/ambient lift; dark mode reuses the chip stack because primary inverts). Pair with `bg-clip-border` so the transparent focus-slot border does not leave a 1px canvas halo between fill and shadow
- When `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add` asks to overwrite `button.tsx` or rewrite `globals.css` tokens, answer **no** — restyle new files afterward per the DESIGN.md playbook
- Load Geist from the `geist` package (`geist/font/sans|mono|pixel`), not `next/font/google` — CSS vars are `--font-geist-sans`, `--font-geist-mono`, `--font-geist-pixel-square`. Display headings use `[--font-weight-semibold:450]` so `font-semibold` remaps via Tailwind's `--font-weight-semibold` token
- Mono type roles (`text-label-12-mono`, etc.) are custom utilities in `globals.css`, not `@theme` text styles — `@theme --text-*` cannot set `font-family`
- Do not port directory routes into Fumadocs — keep data pages on `lib/catalog` + `app/(site)`, prose on `content/docs`; unify via shared search indexes, MDX catalog embeds, and cross-links instead
- Site-wide search uses `createSearchAPI("advanced", { indexes })` (not `createFromSource` alone) so catalog listings can share the Orama index and tag filters with guides
- Do not use Fumadocs `DefaultSearchDialog` with `tags` — its `TagsList` renders outside `DialogContent` and leaks as a permanent top strip; use `components/search-dialog.tsx` (`SiteSearchDialog`) instead

## Catalog agents & registry

- Nested eve-app catalog agents (own `package.json` + `agent/` + `evals/`) are runnable: validate with `npm install && npx eve info` inside the catalog dir (0 errors/warnings) — this beats schema-only checks. Build artifacts (`node_modules/`, `.eve/`, `.cache/`, `var/`, lockfiles) are gitignored **and** skipped by `lib/catalog` file walks — otherwise agent detail pages show thousands of compile/cache files after a local `eve info`

- `shadcn build <path>/registry.json` resolves item file `path`s relative to the repo root (cwd), not the registry file — the generated `.registry/registry.json` can point straight at `catalog/**` with no staging copy
- `shadcn add` shells out to the consumer's detected package manager; a stray parent `pnpm-lock.yaml`/store mismatch breaks installs — smoke-test consumers need their own `package-lock.json` to force npm
- Eve schedules run in task mode and cannot park on HITL approval — scheduled prompts must not call approval-gated tools (`always()`); have them draft instead, and let a live session publish
- Connections used by schedules must be app-scoped (`principalType: "app"`): unattended runs have no user principal and user-scoped Connect auth fails with `principal_required`
- Chat SDK adapters (WhatsApp/Messenger/X) throw at import without provider secrets — do not ship those channel modules in catalog agents that must pass `eve info`; document the snippet in `SETUP.md` instead. First-class channels with lazy `() => process.env…` credentials (Slack Connect, Discord, Telegram, GitHub App) are fine
- Run `npm install` before `npx eve info` in an agent dir — without a local install, npx fetches the _latest_ eve release, which can diverge from the agent's pinned version and fail compile for unrelated reasons (e.g. eve 0.27.0 rejects `anthropic/claude-sonnet-5` compaction metadata that 0.24.x accepts). `pnpm agents:check` does this correctly
- `eveChannel()` requires a config object (`eveChannel({})` or with `auth`); calling it with no args throws on `uploadPolicy`. The HTTP eve channel is already on by default — most catalog agents should omit `channels/eve.ts`

## Staged verification (agents:check / agents:eval / registry:smoke)

- Verification scripts stage agents OUT of the repo (`scripts/lib/stage-agent.mjs` rsyncs to `$EVE_STAGE_DIR`, default `../eve-directory-test`) before installing — in-place runs left ~97MB of `node_modules` per agent (~1GB total) inside `catalog/agents/`, and a parent pnpm workspace would change the module graph vs. what a real consumer gets. Keep the `.gitignore` entries and `lib/catalog` walk exclusions anyway (people still run `eve dev` in-place while authoring); `pnpm agents:clean` drops in-place debris
- rsync `--delete` with `--exclude node_modules/` (and no `--delete-excluded`) protects the staged `node_modules` across re-stages, so repeat `agents:check`/`agents:eval` runs skip the ~45s install
- Catalog evals are hermetic by design: all of them exercise only local tools (JSON stores, memory) and guardrails (`t.parked()`); no connection credentials needed. The only secret is `AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY`, read from `<stage root>/.env.local`. Keep the invariant: new evals must only touch local tools, or `t.skip()` when creds are absent
- Evals run against a live model and are non-deterministic — a HITL-approval eval can fail one run (`observed tools: [load_skill]`) and pass the next with identical code. The dominant flake mode: the model calls `ask_question` to confirm instead of invoking the approval-gated tool (which would park). `run-agent-evals.mjs` retries a failed agent once; deterministic breakage still fails both attempts. Rerun before debugging eval code
- First `eve eval` in a fresh staged copy can stall ~3 minutes on `opening sandbox session "root" on backend "microsandbox"` while the sandbox template builds; it self-recovers ("sandbox template is ready; retrying"). Keep per-agent eval timeouts generous (≥10 min) and don't kill runs that look hung on this line
- `eve eval`'s exit code can be poisoned by teardown noise after every eval already passed (local Workflow queue `socket hang up` retries, quarantined runs from previously interrupted evals). Judge pass/fail from the run's `.eve/evals/<timestamp>/summary.json`, not the process exit code, and delete stale `.eve/.workflow-data` before each staged run — `run-agent-evals.mjs` does both
- Killed/interrupted eval runs leak `msb sandbox` processes (~1GB RAM each, paths under `eve-directory-test/staged/*/node_modules/@superradcompany/microsandbox-*`); sweep them before big eval batches or launches start failing
- Approval-gate evals fail when instructions say "obtain explicit approval before X" — the model calls `ask_question` instead of the gated tool, so the run never parks on the code gate. Write instructions as "call <tool> directly; its in-code gate parks the run for human sign-off; never ask permission in chat; note missing context inside the payload". Keep tool boundaries explicit too: telling the model to call `propose_cancel` eagerly made it treat a `flag_anomaly` finding as a cancellation until the instructions drew the flag-vs-propose line
- `shadcn add` (4.13.x) requires a `tsconfig.json` in the consumer project ("Failed to load tsconfig.json"); registry-smoke scaffolds a stub `{ "compilerOptions": {} }`, which nested eve-app items overwrite with their own on install
- `shadcn build` emits unformatted JSON into `public/r/`, but the committed artifacts are Prettier-formatted — run `pnpm format` after `pnpm registry:build` (or `registry:smoke`, which rebuilds) or `pnpm lint` fails on the regenerated files
